title: How would Third Impact have looked if Gendo didnt add to it
display_title: How would Third Impact have looked if Gendo didnt add to it
wikitext:
[]: How would Third Impact have looked if Gendo didn't add to it             Quote Post by unitM » Sat Jun 06, 2015 10:25 pm Hi guys. As everyone here surely remembers, in EoE, SEELE forced NERV's hand forward after the death of the final angel. It was very explicitly stated that SEELE's intention was to do so and initiate instrumentality using unit 01 as the catalyst. Well they did, but somewhere in between the timeline, Gendo had his moment with Rei and Ritsuko, which concluded with Ritsuko's death and Rei stealing Adam from Gendo and moving into Lilith. This caused Lilith to become GNR and initiate instrumentality, which was already in progress because Shinji was caught in the sky with the MPEs. Let's say Gendo's part was skipped though: how did SEELE plan on starting instrumentality? As pointed out earlier, other characters stated in EoE that SEELE was using 01 to initiate it. Were they just planning on using 01, and if so, how? How would anything have happened, beyond Shinji being flown into the sky, if Lilith was unleashed he way she was, with or without Adam?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Jun 07, 2015 12:47 pm Unit 01 was prized because it could be used as a substitute for Lilith. Presumably, another Evangelion with an S2 Engine could be an Adam substitute? Or, failing that, the Tree of Life AT Field Hubballoo could've done more than summoning the Lance of Longinus, such as summoning the Adam embryo? Perhaps that's why Rei was able to hear Shinji, since it was in her at the time?

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Mon Jun 08, 2015 1:12 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Unit 01 was prized because it could be used as a substitute for Lilith. Presumably, another Evangelion with an S2 Engine could be an Adam substitute? Or, failing that, the Tree of Life AT Field Hubballoo could've done more than summoning the Lance of Longinus, such as summoning the Adam embryo? Perhaps that's why Rei was able to hear Shinji, since it was in her at the time?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jun 08, 2015 1:35 pm After Eva-01 is marked with stigmata, Fuyutsuki wonders if Seele are going to use her as a "yorishiro". To steal Wikipedia's explanation, a yorishiro "in Shinto terminology is an object capable of attracting spirits called kami, thus giving them a physical space to occupy during religious ceremonies". I would assume that the kami is question here is Lilith, and this yorishiro business involves whatever must ceremonially be done to Eva-01 to enable her to function as a Lilith substitute in all the necessary ways. Seele make clear their intent to use the Black Moon as a vessel to receive humanity's souls (going from Fuyutsuki's exposition). As we see later, Lilith has an intimate connection to the Black Moon and is seen funneling souls (which seem to mostly end up within the Moon one way or another) through her own flesh, via the Doors of Guf on her hands. Now, notice the resemblance of the Doors of Guf to stigmata, which Seele made sure Eva-01 received as part of becoming a yorishiro. Eva-01 never made use of those slits for anything, but what if they were SUPPOSED to be artificial Doors of Guf, intended to funnel humanity's souls into the Black Moon in place of Lilith? EDIT: Removed redundant sentence.

[]: Re: How would Third Impact have looked if Gendo didn't add t             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jun 08, 2015 1:38 pm unitM wrote: Let's say Gendo's part was skipped though: how did SEELE plan on starting instrumentality? As pointed out earlier, other characters stated in EoE that SEELE was using 01 to initiate it. Were they just planning on using 01, and if so, how? How would anything have happened, beyond Shinji being flown into the sky, if Lilith was unleashed he way she was, with or without Adam?

[]:             Quote Post by Ispellnogood » Mon Jun 15, 2015 3:24 am Seele’s plan hard to make sense of with just the vague lines we get. All I know is that it was different from Gendo’s one. Which was join all souls into one, using Rei’s/lilith’s global anti-ATF and have a world without at-fields, pain, uncertainty and all that. Like what happened in EoTV, only absurdly in EoE that plan of his seemed to be a last resort he was forced into because of seele’s attack on Nerv. I believe Seele’s goals (at least in the alternate ending EoE) to be something along the lines of converting/rebirthing humanity from beings with the fruit of knowledge into beings with the fruit of life (Angels). Seele sees the angels as the true successors of earth, and changing humanity into Human-angels or in other words Evangelions is what I believe their goal to be. So I think they would have had all the souls of humanity be drawn into the MP evas and divided up evenly between them. And going on the classified info of NGE 2 (PS2 game) about seele’s goals, perhaps with a Seele members soul at the head of each MP Evangelion. I don’t think Rei/Lilith had anything to do with Seele’s plans, I think her anti-ATF was Gendo’s play, it’s only after the lance comes back that they can use Lilith (for some reason) as they originally intended (Somehow). Both Gendo’s and Seele’s plans were happening at the same time. Shinji is thrust into the middle and decides “everybody just die!” Dirtying his hands some more. Shinji has three choices for humanities fate, Orange (Gendo’s plan), Blue (Seele’s Plan) or everybody come back if you wish. Really I think Seele’s plan was always to do with Eva, and converting humanity into human angels. They probably didn’t want Lilith involved, which is probably why she was stabbed with the lance as it prevented her from being involved. That might have been the reason they were pissed when Gendo ordered Rei to use it on Arael, which would’ve allowed Gendo to do his scenario, which involves Lilith/Rei.
